# Sample Bird Data

**Sandhill Crane** https://xeno-canto.org/1046354 (staccato groups, wide harmonic spread) \
**Blue Jay** https://xeno-canto.org/760144 (short brassy burst, wide harmonic spread)

**Barred Owl** https://xeno-canto.org/690957 (distinctive rhythm, medium harmonic spread) \
**Mourning Dove** https://xeno-canto.org/613539 (distinctive rhythm, low harmonic spread)

**Common Loon** https://xeno-canto.org/1046274 (sustained note, low harmonic spread)

---
### Notes
- These were chosen for their relative commonality in North America, as well as their distinctive calls that we will probably all recognize. Audio files are all around 10 seconds long, save the Mourning Dove which includes 2 instances of the call.

- They are grouped above by similarities, to get a sense of what qualities are points of definition.

- Links lead to the full entry of a particular submission. Robust data including location, time, sonograph/spectrogram, contributor info, etc are available from each page.

- Many birds have different sub-calls; a “flight call” can be very different from a standard call. As we prototype, we will stick with just the “call” type from a set like this.
